game_name: The Great Circus Mystery Starring Mickey & Minnie

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile characters and creatures in level environments
danger_signals.meaning: Areas containing threats that must be avoided or defeated
Hazards that damage or kill the player character if contact occurs

danger_signals.visual_form: Specific arena spaces with distinct visual markers (e.g., G24 tileset variants in boss areas)
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger zones requiring skill and resource management to survive

safety_signals.meaning: Player can expand health meter by collecting these items

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct visual markers for magician outfit, firefighter uniform, mountain-climbing gear
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones or reward areas where character gains temporary special abilities and invulnerability to specific hazard types
Safe trading zones where coins can be exchanged for items and upgrades

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mickey Mouse iconic silhouette and character sprite
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Starting positions in levels; presence in cinematics

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pete's character sprite and presence as boss enemy
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears as final boss or mini-boss in various contexts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss arena zones; throne room or command areas
Story progression cutscenes; outfit reward events (Goofy gives mountain-climbing gear in level 4)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Story progression (game is dream/magical land exploration)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six distinct level environments (jungle, circus, various themed zones) with unique tilesets and visual style per stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy types and difficulty scale change per level; NPCs appear at story-appropriate moments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sprite changes to display magician robe, firefighter uniform, or climbing gear; visual effects differ (magic blasts, water streams, grappling hook visibility)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains level-specific interaction abilities (underwater breathing in magician form, fire extinguishing in firefighter form, wall-climbing in gear)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health meter visual feedback; magic meter drains when using magic blasts; water meter drains when using water spray
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character movement may slow or become restricted; magic/water attacks become unavailable until meter refill items are collected

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage clear screen with Japanese 'X Stage' terminology (maintained in Japanese version with color variety)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition to next level; story progression cutscene plays

environmental_storytelling.example: The Jungle stage uses G22-02 tree tilesets and G24 sets for boss areas, creating visual distinction between exploration and boss encounter zones
Heart hidden in first tileset of The Jungle provides environmental world-building detail and reward discovery
Firefighter outfit allows water interaction and fire extinguishing, signaling level themes and puzzle mechanics through equipment visual presentation
Goofy appears in level 4 to gift mountain-climbing gear, positioning allies as quest-givers and world guides

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard platformer locomotion; can grab and spin-throw blocks
Shoots charged magic blasts; controls magic carpets; breathes underwater; magic meter visible and drains with usage
Sprays water streams at enemies; extinguishes fires; creates icicles; water meter visible and drains with usage
Uses grappling hook to attach to objects; hoists/swings upward; grabs and throws enemies with hook
Patrol or idle in level areas
Chase or attack player; can be defeated by jumping on them or grabbing/throwing blocks
Multi-phase combat requiring outfit-specific strategies and resource management

notes: The Great Circus Mystery uses a dream-narrative framing where the player-character awakens at the end, suggesting all world-state visuals communicate a magical dreamscape rather than persistent political situation. Danger is signaled through enemy presence and environmental hazards rather than faction conflict. Safety is communicated through outfit acquisition (visual transformation) and item availability. The game employs outfit-gating as both a mechanical progression system and visual world-state indicator — different outfits enable interaction with environmental puzzle elements, making visual presentation directly tied to available actions.
